tweets.text:
ICYMI: Sinaloa Governor Rubén Rocha Moya and nine other current and former Mexican officials, charged with drug trafficking and weapons offenses:

U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton saying, “The Sinaloa Cartel is a ruthless criminal organization that has flooded this community with dangerous drugs for decades. As the indictment lays bare, the Sinaloa Cartel, and other drug trafficking organizations like it, would not operate as freely or successfully without corrupt politicians and law enforcement officials on their payroll.  The support of corrupt foreign officials for deadly trafficking of drugs must end.  Let these charges send a clear message to all officials around the globe who work with narco-traffickers: no matter your title or position, we are committed to bringing you to justice.”

https://t.co/Kq8iCXBLxx

tweets.text:
NEW: @ICEgov is urging California leaders not to release a suspect accused in a deadly stabbing — as the case reignites the clash over sanctuary policies.

Federal officials say Wilfredo Jose Tortolero-Arriechi, a Venezuelan national in the country illegally, is charged in the December killing of 51-year-old Alberto Rangel inside a San Francisco hospital.  

ICE lodged a detainer after the attack — but is now calling on Governor Gavin Newsom and state officials to ensure he’s not released from custody.

According to DHS, the suspect had previously been encountered by Border Patrol in 2023 and released into the U.S.

Officials say in the weeks leading up to the attack, he allegedly threatened hospital staff before the fatal stabbing.

Acting Assistant Secretary Lauren Bis saying, “If it weren’t for the Biden administration’s reckless open-border policies, Alberto Rangel would still be alive. We are calling on Governor Gavin Newsom to commit to not releasing this murderer from jail. California’s sanctuary policies put American lives at risk. Criminal illegal aliens should not be released from jails back onto our streets to terrorize more innocent Americans. If we work together, we can make America safe again. 7 of the 10 safest cities in the U.S. cooperate with ICE law enforcement.”

The suspect is now facing homicide and weapons charges and remains in custody at the San Francisco County Jail, as federal officials continue pressing California to honor ICE detainers — including for more than 33,000 criminal noncitizens in state custody.
